steps.title: Welcome to Day 4
steps.body: The internet is full of information, but most of it is buried in long articles, reports, and videos. Today you will learn how to use AI to cut through all that — and get straight to what matters.

steps.title: AI as your research assistant
steps.body: AI can summarize a long piece of content, explain a complex topic in plain language, list the pros and cons of any option, and answer follow-up questions. You can learn more in ten minutes than most people do in an hour.

steps.title: One important limit
steps.body: AI knows a lot, but its knowledge has a cut-off date. For recent news or fast-changing topics, double-check with a current source. AI is great for timeless concepts — less reliable for this morning's headlines.

steps.title: A great summary prompt
steps.prompt: Here is an article I pasted below. Summarize it in three bullet points — focus on the main finding, why it matters, and what I should do with this information. [paste article text]

steps.title: What made that prompt strong
steps.body: It told AI: what to read (the pasted content), how to format the answer (three bullet points), and exactly what to focus on (finding, significance, action). Specific instructions get specific answers.

steps.title: Quick check
steps.question: What is the best way to ask AI to explain a complex topic?
options: Explain blockchain like I am completely new to technology. Use a simple everyday analogy.
feedback: This will work but you may get a technical answer that is hard to follow.
Perfect. Saying who you are (a newcomer) and asking for an analogy steers AI toward clarity.
AI does not search the web unless it has a specific tool for that — it draws on what it already knows.
This will produce an overwhelming wall of text. Asking for a specific angle is better.

steps.title: True or false?
steps.question: AI is a reliable source for breaking news that happened today.
feedback: Correct — AI's knowledge has a cut-off date, so today's news may be missing or outdated.
Right. For current events, use a news site. AI is best for topics that do not change quickly.

steps.title: Spot the key instructions
steps.body: Tap the parts of this prompt that make it specific: Summarize in three bullet points, focus on the main finding, and tell me what I should do next.

steps.title: Ask for pros and cons
steps.body: When you are weighing a decision, asking AI for a balanced list of pros and cons is one of its most practical uses. Just give it the option you are considering and any relevant context.

steps.title: Fill in the blank
steps.question: When asking AI to explain a complex topic to you, it helps to say "explain this like I am a ______" to get a simpler answer.
feedback: Exactly. Telling AI your starting point shapes how simple or complex the explanation will be.
Good thinking. The key: tell AI how much you already know so it pitches the answer at the right level.

steps.title: Build a research brief

fields.label: What topic do you want to understand?
fields.placeholder: e.g. how credit card interest works

fields.label: How much do you already know about this?
fields.placeholder: e.g. I understand the basics but not the math

fields.label: How do you want the answer?
fields.placeholder: e.g. a short summary with three key takeaways

steps.template: Explain {{topic}} to me. My current knowledge level: {{level}}. Please give me {{format}}.

steps.title: Copy your research prompt
steps.body: Copy this prompt and paste it into your AI tool to see the explanation it produces.

steps.title: Paste what you got back
steps.body: Paste the explanation AI gave you here to save it to your work.

steps.title: One more check
steps.question: You found a long report about a new health trend. How do you get the most useful summary?
options: Ask AI: What is this about?
Ask AI to summarize in three bullet points covering the main finding, evidence, and what to do with the information — then paste the report.
Ask AI: Is this good for me?
Trust AI's summary without checking anything else.
feedback: Too vague — you will get a general overview without the specific angles you care about.
Great. Structured prompts with pasted content and specific angles produce the most useful summaries.
AI cannot evaluate personal health decisions — only a professional who knows you can do that.
For health information, it is always worth verifying with a qualified source.

steps.title: Match the goal to the right prompt

pairs.right: Explain this like I am a beginner
Give me the pros and cons

questions.question: Why should you not rely on AI for today's news?
options: AI does not like news topics
AI's knowledge has a cut-off date so recent events may be missing

questions.question: Saying 'explain this like I am a beginner' usually makes AI answers clearer.
To get a focused summary, ask AI for a specific number of ______ points.

steps.title: Day 4 complete
steps.body: You can now use AI to summarize, explain, and research — turning hours of reading into minutes of clarity. That is a serious advantage. See you tomorrow.
